name: Federated Employees
description_short: Member-owned credit union serving Federated Insurance employees and their families since 1934, offering competitive savings, loans, and financial services.
description_long:
Federated Employees Credit Union (FECU) was established on January 19, 1934, by 26 Federated Insurance employees as a member-owned, not-for-profit cooperative. It operates as a traditional credit union with two physical locations in Owatonna, Minnesota, serving exclusively Federated Insurance Companies employees, retirees, and their extended family members (spouses, children, grandchildren, parents, in-laws, and siblings).

FECU offers core banking services including savings accounts, certificate of deposit products (such as $ave-n-Grow 9-Month Term Share Certificates), checking and spending accounts, borrowing/loan products, and account management tools. The credit union actively promotes financial education through initiatives like America Saves Week and provides member discounts on tax preparation services. Membership requires minimal barriers to entry—only a $5 share deposit into a savings account.

The primary distinction between FECU and traditional banks is its cooperative structure and member-ownership model. Members share in profits through lower loan rates, higher deposit rates, and reduced or eliminated fees. The Board of Directors consists of volunteer members elected by the membership, ensuring leadership has direct member interests rather than stockholder profits as priority. This fundamentally differs from stock-based financial institutions where profits flow to external investors.

FECU is best suited for Federated Insurance employees and their immediate and extended family members seeking a member-centric alternative to commercial banks. The main limitation is strict membership eligibility—general consumers cannot join. Service hours are limited (7:30 AM–4:30 PM weekdays at main office, 9:00 AM–3:30 PM at downtown office), with no weekend access. The credit union does not appear to offer comprehensive digital banking services or nationwide branch networks typical of larger institutions. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Choice Savings accounts with recurring deposit options
Member discounts on financial products (TurboTax, tax refund estimation)
Financial education programs (America Saves Week, Credit Union Youth Month)
Youth savings products and youth financial education contests
Board of Directors election and member voting rights
Profit-sharing through lower rates and reduced fees
pros: Member-owned cooperative structure returns profits to members through lower loan rates, higher savings rates, and reduced fees
Volunteer Board of Directors elected by members ensures leadership prioritizes member service over stockholder profits
Minimal barrier to membership—only $5 share deposit required to become a member-owner
No-fee or low-fee services available to members after credit union reaches capital reserve levels
Established 90+ year history (since 1934) demonstrating stability and long-term commitment to members
Active member benefits including exclusive discounts (TurboTax calculator), America Saves Week participation, and youth savings programs
Personalized service through local offices with direct phone access (507-455-5430, 507-455-5116)
cons: Highly restricted membership eligibility limited to Federated Insurance employees, retirees, and their immediate/extended family—general public cannot join
Limited service hours with no weekend availability (Monday–Friday only, closing as early as 3:30 PM at downtown location)
Only two physical branch locations in Owatonna, Minnesota, with no indication of ATM networks or online banking capabilities
Small asset base and membership compared to national banks or credit unions limits product depth and technological innovation
Membership validation requires Federated Insurance employee ID number or full legal name of eligible employee/retiree, creating administrative friction
best_for: Federated Insurance employees and retirees seeking member-owned financial services with profit-sharing benefits
Families of Federated Insurance employees (spouses, children, grandchildren, parents, in-laws, siblings) who qualify for membership
Members prioritizing cooperative banking principles and lower fees over technology-driven banking convenience
Conservative savers interested in certificate products like the $ave-n-Grow 9-Month Term Share Certificate with member incentives
diagnosis: FECU is exclusively designed for Federated Insurance employees and their families seeking a traditional, member-owned alternative to commercial banks with cooperative profit-sharing benefits. The critical caveat is that general consumers cannot join—membership eligibility is strictly limited to Federated Insurance company employees, retirees, and their extended family, making this institution inaccessible to the general public.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
